title: payera coriacea
intro: Payera coriacea, also known as the West Indian coral tree, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Payera coriacea include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, such as wounds and skin conditions. Payera coriacea can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
